note: Human-readable release notes the desktop shows in Settings → Advanced → Version. Newest first. Set "critical": true on a release that fixes data loss, a security issue, or a breaking sync change so it is called out prominently and the desktop can nudge harder.

entries.headline: First public release
notes: Every app is a real animated GIF you own — drop it on your Home Screen and it runs, and sharing the file shares the whole app with its saved data inside.
Peer-to-peer Meetings that scale to large rooms on a self-healing mesh, with no server in the middle — and you can run any app live inside a meeting.
Any app goes multiplayer with one invite link (direct WebRTC, relay fallback); the link is yours by a secret only your app holds.
Fully offline-capable: your computer and its apps run in airplane mode. Back up a whole computer as one GIF and boot it anywhere.
Ten themed computers (the numbered subdomains), provenance signing, and per-person AI/API keys that apps can use but never see.
Two-player games (Tic-Tac-Toe, Connect Four) ask before New game clears the board — the other player consents, or you confirm your own reset.
Version panel: pin or roll back to any release. Switching a build rebuilds the built-in default apps to that build, and the data saved inside them carries over.
